title: Replace nonexistent `bd worktree`/`bd merge-slot` verbs in shift skills with `git worktree add` + Cage Cashier write-queue

Agent charters (agents/the-dealer.md:19, the-pit-boss.md:19) already use the correct `git worktree add`, so this is a doc-only divergence. Cheap, high-trust, unblocks honest worktree guidance.

Correct form (`git worktree add`; export BEADS_DIR) already used in agents/the-dealer.md:19 and agents/the-pit-boss.md:19, confirming this is a pure doc-div…

(Source: parallel-agents-audit workflow, 2026-06-16.)

(Source: parallel-agents-audit workflow, 2026-06-16.)
acceptance_criteria:
In driver/src/main.ts, after isHelpRequested/validateBackend and resolveConfig, build the composition root and run a real shift:

1. Construct deps: WriteQueue + Beads(exec, queue), Journal (seeded via `Journal.replay(path)`), RunLog, BudgetLedger(cfg.budgetUsd, cfg.budgetTokens), and a dealer + a SEPARATE auditor adapter instance selected by cfg.backend (ClaudeAdapter for claude, CodexAdapter for codex; api may reuse ClaudeAdapter or be explicitly handled). exec must be the real ExecFn (injectable for tests).

Build the tick closure: `runShift` requires `tick: (iteration) => Promise<TickOutcome>` where TickOutcome = { closedIds: string[]; empty: boolean }, but runSingleBeadTick takes a TickDeps object and returns TickResult ({ claimed?, closed?, skipped?, reason? }). The composition root MUST wrap runSingleBeadTick in a closure that closes over the constructed TickDeps and maps its result to TickOutcome — empty:true when reason === 'queue-empty', closedIds from a non-null `closed` (else []). Do not pass runSingleBeadTick to runShift unadapted (the types do not match).
3. Call runShift with maxIterations=cfg.maxIterations and noProgressWindow=cfg.noProgressWindow, print the ShiftReport (iterations, closed, stopped), and return a sensible exit code (0 on a clean stop; non-zero on budget breach or thrown error).
4. main() becomes async (Promise<number>); update the `import.meta.url` direct-invoke guard to await it, and keep the existing exported pure helpers (isHelpRequested/usage/validateBackend) so main.test.ts still imports them.

Tests: add a test that drives main() with a MockAdapter seam (backend-to-adapter selection overridable, e.g. via an injected adapter factory or exec stub) and asserts a CAPPED shift runs and the printed/returned ShiftReport reports the expected closed bead ids. close_reason: Driver main.ts now runs a real capped K=1 shift (composition root + runShift + TickOutcome mapping + ShiftReport); exec injectable. Dealer TDD 77 pass, independent Floor Auditor PASS, gate GREEN.

title: shift-loop.sh: detect a bead stalled in_progress/blocked (closed-count flat) and inject an escalation prompt before clocking out
description:
shift-loop.sh (the wired-in Stop-hook engine, the 'continuous' path the owner wants) has NO stuck-bead escalation — night-shift.sh has FIVE_TO_NINE_STUCK_BEAD_THRESHOLD/MODEL retry-with-bigger-model (night-shift.sh:121-204), but the Stop loop only watches the closed count, so a bead bouncing in_progress/blocked without closing is invisible until 3 stalls and is never escalated or surfaced. For a continuous in-session loop this means silent spinning. Edits the live Stop-hook, so off-loop.

SELF-ENGINE HAZARD: edits the live loop engine (night-shift.sh / shift-loop.sh / gate). Deferred from autonomous claiming — work in a controlled session, not the live loop.

Audit verify notes: Premise verified real. hooks/shift-loop.sh (the Stop-hook engine) has only a closed-count no-progress guard (lines 60-74); it has no concept of a bead spinning in_progress/blocked. night-shift.sh DOES have full stuck-bead escalation (lines 121-204: FIVE_TO_NINE_STUCK_BEAD_THRESHOLD/MODEL, env-var model bump, surface-after-retry). So a continuous in-session shift can silently spin on one bead until the closed-count stall fires and it just clocks out, with no escalation. Genuine gap, not gold-plating; owner wants the continuous path. self_engine_hazard=true is CORRECT (edits hooks/shift-loop.sh,…

(Source: parallel-agents-audit workflow, 2026-06-16.)
acceptance_criteria: In hooks/shift-loop.sh, when ready==0 OR the closed-count snapshot is flat for >= FIVE_TO_NINE_STUCK_BEAD_THRESHOLD (default 1) consecutive Stop-hook fires WHILE f9_bd_count_status in_progress (or blocked) is > 0, treat the shift as stuck rather than immediately clocking out. On the first such detection, persist an escalation marker in state_dir (e.g. stuck.count / stuck.escalated) and block() once with an escalation reason: instruct the in-session model to resume the stuck in_progress/blocked bead directly (not bd ready --claim), bump the relevant role one tier (state CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=${FIVE_TO_NINE_STUCK_BEAD_MODEL:-opus} in the prompt text, since a Stop hook cannot set env for the running model), and surface the exact gate failure. If the next fire shows the same stall persists after escalation, allow_stop() and emit an f9_warn surfacing the stuck bead to the human (do not block forever). Honor the existing iteration cap and no-progress guard precedence. Reuse f9_bd_count_status (in_progress/blocked) and the existing closed.snapshot pattern; do NOT claim to capture the agent's claimed bead id (the Stop hook has no such signal). Add a case to tests/smoke-shift.sh (or a new test wired into tests/validate-plugin.sh) exercising the stall->escalate->surface transition via a stubbed bd. bash tests/validate-plugin.sh exits 0.

title: night-shift.sh: add a --budget-usd / iteration-proxy breaker that stops the loop before the next agent dispatch
description:
The ~15x cost rule (SKILL.md:75-76, pit-boss:24) is asserted but unenforced in the shipped bash engine — there is no per-iteration cost accounting or budget breaker, so enabling any fan-out risks runaway spend. The driver already models this (BudgetLedger.breached() in driver/src/observability.ts, checked by runShift), but night-shift.sh has only iteration/no-progress/stuck guards. Owner wants continuous looping; a continuous loop with no spend ceiling is the unguarded-loop hazard AGENTS.md warns against. Edits the live engine, so must be done off the live loop.

SELF-ENGINE HAZARD: edits the live loop engine (night-shift.sh / shift-loop.sh / gate). Deferred from autonomous claiming — work in a controlled session, not the live loop.

Audit verify notes: Verified against the tree. GAP IS REAL: night-shift.sh (208 lines) guards iterations/QUEUE-EMPTY/no-progress/stuck-bead but has zero cost or budget accounting (grep for budget/cost/usd hits only the --dry-run help). DRIVER PARITY claim is accurate: driver/src/observability.ts:22 class BudgetLedger, breached() at L38-39, config.ts:45 defaults budgetUsd to $5 metered-api, main.ts:18 parses --budget-usd — so the bash engine genuinely lags a shipped driver capability. CITATIONS check out: SKILL.md L75-76 and the-pit-boss.md L24 both assert the ~15x dispatch cost; it is unenforced in bash. SELF_ENG…

(Source: parallel-agents-audit workflow, 2026-06-16.)
acceptance_criteria: scripts/night-shift.sh accepts a `--budget-usd N` flag and a `FIVE_TO_NINE_BUDGET_USD` env var (flag wins; empty/0 = uncapped, preserving current behavior). Because run_agent currently invokes `claude -p ... --dangerously-skip-permissions` with no JSON output and captures no cost data, the breaker uses a documented iteration-count proxy by default (a per-iteration USD estimate, defaulting such that the cap maps to a finite iteration count) and may use real observed cost only if run_agent is changed to capture it; either way the chosen mechanism and its conversion are documented in the `--help`/usage header. When cumulative estimated (or observed) cost reaches/exceeds the cap, the loop logs a single `BUDGET-BREACHED` line (via f9_warn/f9_log) and `break`s BEFORE invoking run_agent for the next iteration — the cap must never be exceeded by an extra dispatch. The existing guards (max-iterations, QUEUE-EMPTY, no-progress stall, stuck-bead escalation) are unchanged. A new case added to the existing, validate-wired tests/night-shift-test.sh (which stubs bd and the agent cmd) drives the loop with a low `--budget-usd` and asserts: (a) a BUDGET-BREACHED line is emitted, and (b) run_agent/the agent stub is NOT invoked after the cap is hit (e.g. via an invocation-count sentinel file). `bash tests/validate-plugin.sh` exits 0.

title: Gate precision: classifier scans full command TEXT, false-blocks safe tools whose args quote a gated action
description: The PreToolUse irreversible-gate classifies Bash command TEXT by substring. A harmless command whose ARGUMENTS merely describe a gated outward action (a bd note, a git commit -m message, a bd remember) is falsely blocked. Seen twice on 2026-06-16: a bd remember and a bd update --notes were blocked solely because the note text quoted an earlier gated command. For a hands-off loop this is a stall risk — the crew records WHY it stopped, and that record trips the gate. Directly impacts owner goal #4 (autonomy).
design: Anchor matches to the executed verb (argv[0] + action), not arbitrary string args to known-safe tools. Options: (a) allowlist safe tools (bd, git commit -m, echo, printf) whose quoted args are not scanned; (b) parse argv and match only the command in command position; (c) ignore text inside single/double-quoted heredoc bodies. Keep fail-closed for genuine outward verbs.
acceptance_criteria: A bd remember / git commit -m whose message quotes a gated action runs without a gate block, while a real gated outward command is still blocked; gate-cases.txt gains both cases; node --test + validate-plugin.sh GREEN.
notes: SECURITY CAVEAT found while scoping (changes the approach): the naive fix — allowlist safe tools (bd, git commit -m) and stop scanning their string args — is UNSAFE. A safe tool's argument can contain command substitution ($(...) or backticks) that EXECUTES an arbitrary outward command, so the classifier must keep detecting substitution even inside safe-tool args. A defensible narrowing: allow ONLY when the flagged phrase sits inside a SINGLE-quoted literal (single quotes block substitution in bash) AND the verb is a known-safe tool; double-quoted/unquoted stays blocked (can interpolate). This makes mu0.5 a careful security change, not a quick autonomous fix. Note the asymmetry: the current over-broad behavior fails CLOSED (annoying but safe); a wrong narrowing fails OPEN (dangerous). Recommend Eye-in-the-Sky design + human review before touching the gate. Left OPEN + escalated; the crew self-restrains on security by design.

title: Codex plugin add can't resolve a plugin at the marketplace ROOT (path ".") — needs subdir layout
description: Finding (codex-cli 0.140.0): Codex reads its marketplace from .agents/plugins/marketplace.json and resolves each plugin's source.path RELATIVE TO THE MARKETPLACE ROOT. Every OpenAI plugin lives in a SUBDIR (./plugins/<name>); a plugin at '.' (repo root, which also hosts .agents/) yields zero plugins — 'codex plugin add the-5-to-9@the-5-to-9' fails 'plugin not found in marketplace'. authentication policy (ON_USE/ON_INSTALL) is NOT the cause. The structural gate (manifest valid + skills declared) passes, but the empirical install does not.
design: Decision needed: expose a Codex plugin SUBDIR (e.g. .agents/plugins/the-5-to-9/ with its own .codex-plugin/plugin.json + skills) WITHOUT duplicating the canonical skills/ (drift risk) and WITHOUT relying on symlinks (Windows/Git-Bash checkouts unreliable per repo conventions). Options: (a) a generator script that builds the subdir from canonical skills as a release step + a gate check it's in sync; (b) accept a symlink with a documented Windows caveat; (c) move canonical skills into the subdir and point Claude at it. Pick (a) unless simpler.
acceptance_criteria: codex plugin marketplace add <repo> then codex plugin add the-5-to-9@the-5-to-9 installs the plugin and 'codex plugin list' shows it with its skills; no skills duplicated out of sync; validate-plugin.sh GREEN incl. a sync check.
close_reason: Resolved. Codex resolves plugins only from a subdir (root '.' fails) and copies just that subdir into its cache, so skills must live inside it. Built .agents/plugins/the-5-to-9/ (own manifest + byte-synced skills copy), pointed marketplace source.path at it, gate enforces sync (diff -r). Verified end-to-end: codex plugin add installs+enables and the 3 skills land in cache. No symlink, no build step.

title: Gate no-jq fallback: also decode unicode whitespace escapes (\u000a/\u0009/\u000d)
description: jnx.1 decoded literal \n/\t/\r in the bash+no-jq gate fallback. A newline encoded as \u000a (instead of \n) would still slip the no-node+no-jq path, since the sed extractor leaves it literal and the deny-list keyword keeps a non-boundary neighbor. Node/jq paths already handle it (JSON decode). Low priority: the fallback is rare and the threat model is accidental crew actions, not crafted payloads, but decoding the three control-char unicode escapes to whitespace closes the variant.
acceptance_criteria: irreversible-gate.sh bash fallback maps \u000a/\u0009/\u000d (case-insensitive) to whitespace before segmentation; a gate-cases row using a unicode-escaped newline before an outward verb DENYs on the bash+no-jq path; gate corpus green on both paths.
